title: Bridge Deck Vibration Adaptive Damper Controller
description: Closed-loop adaptive magnetorheological damper system that monitors bridge deck acceleration via a distributed ADXL345 array, computes real-time RMS using a windowed buffer, and drives a PWM-controlled MR damper through a PID loop to suppress resonance and keep DAF below 1.5 under pedestrian and wind excitation.
objective: Maintain bridge deck RMS acceleration below 0.5 g and reduce dynamic amplification factor toward 1.0; log all events to SD and alarm on >2.5 g structural exceedance.
constraints: Arduino Mega 2560; 12 V 5 A supply; MR coil ≤2 A; FoS ≥2.0 on structural members; SD logging mandatory; BOM <$80
supporting_fact_titles: Structural Resonance and Dynamic Amplification Factor
design_element_titles: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller

element_usage_notes.ADXL345 Three-Axis Accelerometer Node Ar: Provides per-node RMS-g as process variable to the outer PID loop
element_usage_notes.PWM-Driven Magnetorheological Damper: Receives PID output as 0-255 PWM; higher duty increases fluid viscosity and damping force
element_usage_notes.SPI SD Card Datalogger Shield: 10 Hz CSV logging; fault events trigger immediate sync to prevent data loss on power failure
element_usage_notes.16x2 I2C LCD Status Panel: Operator HMI; top row live RMS, bottom row damper duty and fault state
element_usage_notes.Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controlle: Outer loop regulates RMS against 0.5 g setpoint; inner loop regulates rate-of-change of acceleration

element_interactions.to: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller
element_interactions.description: Windowed RMS acceleration fed as outer-loop process variable

element_interactions.from: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller
element_interactions.description: PID output clipped to 0-255 sets MR coil current via PWM
Raw and RMS acceleration logged at 10 Hz

element_interactions.from: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller
element_interactions.description: Setpoint, PV, output percent displayed for operator
